processing.title: We’re organizing your content into a mind map...
processing.bullet1: Instead of just summarizing, we first analyze the flow, steps and branches.
processing.bullet2: Depending on the length of the video, this can take tens of seconds or more.
processing.bullet3: As long as you keep this page open, the process will continue automatically.

items: Mark 3 important nodes first while reading
Add 1-2 notes to key nodes
Save your output via share link or PDF/Markdown export

modal.bullet1: The longer the video, the longer the processing time may be.
modal.bullet2: Once started, this cannot be undone and used credits are non-refundable.
modal.bullet3: We recommend keeping this page open while processing is in progress.

YoutubeScriptDialog.title: How to get a YouTube transcript
YoutubeScriptDialog.subtitle: You can copy the full transcript in under a minute.
YoutubeScriptDialog.gifAlt: How to quickly copy a YouTube transcript

guide.intro: Brify does not auto-extract transcripts from a YouTube URL because we respect YouTube's policies. Instead, you can copy the full transcript quickly and reliably with the method below.
guide.desktopOnly: Note: On mobile, YouTube may not fully support transcript copy workflows. We recommend doing this on desktop.
guide.stepsTitle: The easiest way to copy it
guide.step1: Click the first word in the transcript (or start selecting there)
guide.step3: Hold Shift and click the last character
guide.step4: Once everything is selected, copy and paste it into the input box
guide.encourage: Don’t let useful videos slip by just because you’re short on time. Paste the transcript and turn key ideas into a clear mind map in minutes.

YoutubeScriptDialog.imagePlaceholder: You can place a related image here.

5.title: Keep holding Shift
5.description: Do not release it, and click the last word right away.

8.title: Paste it into the input field

CreditConfirmModal.splitNotice: This input is long, so we’ll generate {count} structure maps first and then merge them into one.

processingMessages.2: Please wait just a little longer.
processingMessages.3: Long content is fine. We’re building it step by step.
processingMessages.4: We’re organizing the key flow right now.

ScriptHelpSection.headerTitle: How to copy a YouTube transcript

sectionA.title: Getting a transcript from YouTube on desktop
sectionA.description: Follow these steps to copy the full transcript in about 1–2 minutes.

step1.title: Click the "···" or "More" button below the video.
step1.body: Look under the video title, next to the Like/Share buttons, for the three dots (···) or a "More" button.
step1.imageAlt: Location of the More (···) button on the YouTube watch page.

step2.title: In the menu, choose "Show transcript" or "Transcript".
step2.body: In the menu that opens, click the item named "Show transcript", "Captions", or "Transcript".
step2.imageAlt: YouTube menu showing the Show transcript option.

step3.title: Select the entire transcript on the right and copy it.
step3.tip1: 1. Click once on the first line of the transcript.
step3.tip2Part1: 2. Scroll all the way down, then
step3.tip2Part2: click the last line once to select the entire transcript.
step3.tip3: 3. Press Ctrl + C (or ⌘ + C on Mac) to copy.
step3.tipNote: ※ Using Ctrl + A selects the whole page, so we don’t recommend it when you only want the transcript.
step3.imageAlt: Selecting the entire transcript in the YouTube transcript panel.

step4.title: Paste it into the input box on the left of this page.
step4.body: Click the "Paste video script" input area, then press Ctrl + V (or ⌘ + V on Mac) to paste. Now you’re ready.
step4.imageAlt: Pasting text into the video script input field.

sectionB.title: Want to use subtitles or an editing tool instead?
sectionB.description: If you already have a project in progress, you can simply copy the text and paste it here.

case1.title: Copying subtitles or script from an editor (Premiere, CapCut, etc.)
case1.body: Select all subtitle text from the timeline, copy it, and paste it into the input on the left.

case2.title: Opening subtitle files (srt, vtt, etc.) as text
case2.body: Open the srt/vtt file in a text editor like Notepad. It will contain timestamps with dialogue. Copy the lines you need and paste them here.

case3.title: Using a script document you’ve already written
case3.body: It doesn’t matter where your script lives—blog, Word, Notion, etc. Just copy the full text and paste it as-is.

faq.title: Q. I don’t see a "Show transcript" / "Transcript" menu.
faq.item1: Some videos may hide the transcript at the creator’s choice.
faq.item2: There may be no subtitles at all, or it might be a live/real-time stream.
faq.item3: In that case, you’ll need to type it manually while listening, or use a separate subtitle generation tool.

DemoPage.title: Read a famous talk as a structure map

intro.intro: We prepared a well-known TED talk as a structure map example.
intro.speaker: Simon Sinek is a globally influential leadership expert and author. His TED talk, 'How Great Leaders Inspire Action,' is one of the most-watched talks and explains how brands like Apple succeed.
intro.lead: The Golden Circle is one of the most widely known ideas in leadership and branding.
intro.body1: There are so many valuable talks people recommend, but it is hard to find the time to watch them all.
intro.body2: That is why we built Brify.
intro.body3: Even without watching the YouTube video, you can paste in the script and understand the core ideas structurally in just a few minutes, in your own language.

preview.title: Mind Map Preview
preview.helper: This example is a fixed preview screen. Press the button to explore the mind map directly.

toasts.demoFinished: The demo is over. Returning to the home page.

shareDialog.title: This mind map is for demo only
shareDialog.description: In the full service, you can share your mind map by link and view it together with others. Start using Brify to create a share link for your own map.

notes.helper: You can jot down quick thoughts while reading important nodes.
notes.placeholder: Write down what comes to mind from this node.

items.text: The meal timing makes the daily fasting rhythm easy to follow at a glance.
A key takeaway is that he avoids OMAD to maintain weight, not because of hunger.

terms.helper: AI automatically extracts key terms to make the content easier to understand.

items.meaning: Used as a quality standard for animal-based foods in the video.
A sourcing standard for eggs and livestock that is emphasized throughout the routine.

expand.label: Expand / Collapse
expand.description: Use the + button to open child nodes

edit.title: Switch to Edit mode before editing
edit.description: You're currently in View mode. Switch to Edit mode at the top before changing nodes or structure.
edit.calloutDescription: Start here when you want to edit the map.

mouse.title: Here is how to move around the map
mouse.description: Use right-drag with a mouse, move with two fingers on a trackpad, or navigate with the mini map.
mouse.calloutDescription: Use right-drag, two fingers, or the mini map to get where you want quickly.

trackpad.title: Use two fingers on a trackpad to move the map
trackpad.description: On a laptop trackpad, slide with two fingers to pan across the map naturally.
trackpad.calloutDescription: Use a two-finger gesture to move around.

context.title: Select a node, then right click
context.description: Select a node first, then right click to open a context menu with the available actions.
context.calloutDescription: The menu opens after selecting a node.

highlight.title: Use highlight to mark important parts
highlight.description: Click the middle icon to highlight a node. Mark important parts so they are much easier to revisit later.
highlight.calloutDescription: Highlight key parts while you read.

expand.title: Expand and collapse nodes to read more comfortably
expand.description: Click the + button inside the circle to reveal child content. You can collapse it again and follow only what you need, which makes the Structure Map much easier to read.
expand.calloutDescription: Click the + button to reveal child content.

terms.title: AI organizes difficult terms for you
terms.description: In the Terms tab on the left panel, AI can gather unfamiliar words from the content and explain them clearly.
terms.calloutDescription: AI finds difficult terms and organizes them for you.

shortcuts.title: It gets much easier once you remember the shortcuts
shortcuts.description: Once you remember the most useful shortcuts, working with a Structure Map becomes much faster. You can always open this again from More > Shortcuts.

edit.title: Editing is ready on mobile by default
edit.description: On mobile, the Structure Map opens in an editable state from the start.
edit.calloutDescription: You can start editing right away on mobile.

move.title: You can drag the map directly on mobile
move.description: On mobile, you do not need a separate move mode. Just drag with one finger on empty space or away from the node controls to pan the map.
move.calloutDescription: Try dragging with one finger to move the map.

select.title: Tap a node to select it, and long-press to open the edit menu
select.description: On mobile, a single tap selects a node. The small quick actions next to the node are available right away, and in Edit mode the bottom edit bar opens when you long-press.
select.calloutDescription: Tap a node to select it, then long-press when you want the edit menu.

highlight.title: Highlight important nodes as you read
highlight.description: When a node is selected, small quick-action buttons appear next to it. Use the highlight button there to mark important parts.
highlight.calloutDescription: Select a node, then tap the highlight button.

expand.title: Expand and collapse nodes to read more comfortably
expand.description: Tap the + button inside the circle to reveal child content. You can collapse it again and follow only what you need, which makes the Structure Map much easier to read.
expand.calloutDescription: Tap the + button to reveal child content.

panel.title: Open the left panel for more context
panel.description: Tap the left panel button to open the Info, Notes, and Terms tabs. While reading the Structure Map, you can quickly check whatever you need there.
panel.calloutDescription: This button opens the Info, Notes, and Terms tabs.

terms.title: AI organizes difficult terms for you
terms.description: In the Terms tab on the left panel, AI can gather unfamiliar words from the content and explain them clearly.
terms.calloutDescription: You can review difficult terms here.

CreatorSection.label: Why I built Brify
CreatorSection.name: Lina
CreatorSection.quote: “There is always more to read, while there is less time to decide. I built Brify to help people read documents where missing something carries real risk, faster and more accurately. Not through summary, but through structure, so you can grasp both the key points and the details together.”

MapLibrarySection.title: Structure complex information and grasp the flow and key points at a glance
MapLibrarySection.subtitle: Don’t stop at collecting scattered information. Compare it, organize it, and turn it into a standard you can actually rely on in your own judgment.

tags.title: Tag Organization
tags.description: Group mind maps by theme so it becomes easier to organize and review related ideas.

merge.title: Merge Maps
merge.description: Connect multiple channels and sources into one flow so you can see the bigger picture.

edit.title: Edit Freely
edit.description: Edit your mind map freely in your own way.

read.title: Highlight and Notes
read.description: Return to the important parts and keep your focus even when different viewpoints conflict.

share.title: Share and Download
share.description: Keep the standards and insights you organized accessible as links or images.

one.quote: I tested it with a theology paper full of Hebrew technical terms and complex chiastic structures. I was amazed by how clearly it organized the hierarchy of the core model and even subtle textual nuances. The structure map quality was outstanding.

two.quote: When I turned course documents into structure maps, the experimental flow and the relationships between key concepts became clear at a glance. When I go back to long documents, I can quickly tell where to look first, and my efficiency has improved a lot.

three.quote: When reviewing case law and legal opinions, the structure map was far more practical than a simple summary. Issues, reasoning, and conclusions were organized together, so I could quickly find what I needed and put it to use right away.

LandingFeatureListSection.title: Core Brify Features
LandingFeatureListSection.subtitle: A single workflow from reading to refining and sharing.
items: Supports multiple input formats: text, PDF, and DOCX
Fast, accurate structure map generation
Stable generation with automatic split-and-merge for large inputs
Direct map editing with node highlights and notes
Recheck original source locations and manage maps with tags and reading status
Share links and export results as PDF or Markdown

LandingComparisonSection.title: How is it different from NotebookLM?
LandingComparisonSection.subtitle: If your priority is quick understanding, NotebookLM may fit better. If you need to keep working on and managing the result directly, Brify may fit better.

rows.feature: Mark important nodes as you read
Add notes to nodes as you read
Check the original source location and revise directly from the node

notebooklm.label: Original source only through chat answers; nodes cannot be edited

rows.feature: Organize, reopen, and manage structure maps with tags and reading status
rows.detail: You can mark each structure map as In progress, Important, or Reviewed and keep managing it as part of your workflow.

rows.feature: Combine multiple structure maps and view them in a single structure map
rows.detail: Brify lets you merge generated structure maps and reorganize them into a single flow.

notebooklm.label: Can explore combined sources, but no structure-map merge flow

rows.feature: Generate reliably with automatic split-and-merge for large inputs
rows.detail: Large text is automatically split, generated, and merged again to reduce mid-process failures and stabilize final output.

notebooklm.label: You can split sources manually, but there is no automatic split-and-merge workflow

notebooklm.title: NotebookLM
notebooklm.description: Best for asking questions across multiple materials and understanding them quickly.
items: Bring multiple materials into one notebook and explore them through questions
Use citations to quickly jump back to the relevant source passages
limitations: Not designed for directly editing the generated structure map or marking important parts
No built-in flow for organizing structure maps themselves with tags and reading status

brify.title: Brify
brify.description: Brify is not just a summary of the key points. It keeps the location of the details you may need to revisit inside the structure map.
items: Freely edit the structure map and mark what matters
Jump back to the original text from the map and keep refining
Turn the map into share links, PDF, or Markdown outputs
Reopen and manage maps with tags and reading status

bestFit.title: Brify fits better when this is part of your regular workflow
bestFit.subtitle: The more often you need to highlight, compare, revisit the original text, and turn what you read into shareable outputs, the more useful Brify becomes.
items: When you read long documents often and need to revisit exact details later
When you need to reorganize what you read in your own way after reading multiple documents
When your work does not end at reading but continues into sharing and deliverables
When you need to turn AI output into your own work product instead of leaving it as-is
When this is not a one-off task, but something you do repeatedly

notebooklm.label: NotebookLM
notebooklm.text: A better fit if your priority is asking questions and understanding materials quickly.

brify.label: Brify
brify.text: A better fit if you want a hands-on structure map—one where you can mark nodes, add notes as you read, revise what matters, and turn it into an output.

LandingUsageGuideSection.title: App Usage Guide
LandingUsageGuideSection.subtitle: From input to sharing, here is the real 6-step workflow.
LandingUsageGuideSection.flowAlt: Visual guide of the Brify usage flow

steps.title: Add document or text
steps.description: Paste text directly, or upload a PDF/DOCX file.

steps.title: Choose output language
steps.description: Select the language you want for the result.

steps.title: Generate structure map
steps.description: Click generate to start processing, then move to the result view when it is ready.

steps.description: Highlight key nodes, add notes, and edit the structure where needed.

steps.title: Manage tags and reading status
steps.description: Organize maps with tags and reading status so you can reopen and continue later.

steps.title: Share and export outputs
steps.description: Create a share link and export your result as PDF or Markdown.

LandingUsageGuideSection.badge: Built for doing, not just browsing
LandingUsageGuideSection.footnote: This flow is designed to help you create your own map right away, not just look at examples.

LandingAudienceSection.title: Brify is especially useful for these kinds of users
LandingAudienceSection.subtitle: The more often you read long documents, revisit specific details, and turn what you read into reusable outputs, the more valuable Brify becomes.

items.title: Researchers and graduate students
items.description: When you need to read papers, specialist materials, or foreign-language documents while keeping both the main idea and the important details together

items.title: Legal, policy, and review work
items.description: When you need to locate issues and key information quickly in long documents, then reopen them and continue reviewing

items.title: Strategy, planning, and research roles
items.description: When you need to read multiple reports and source materials, structure them in your own way, and turn them into shareable outputs

items.title: Professionals who regularly read dense documents
items.description: When you want to keep marking, annotating, and managing reading status instead of simply reading once and moving on

LandingAudienceSection.footnote: Brify fits best when your documents are not one-time reads, but materials you reopen, refine, and reuse.

LandingOutcomeSection.title: With consistent use of Brify, this is how you change
LandingOutcomeSection.subtitle: With the same amount of time, your depth of understanding and speed of judgment both improve.
LandingOutcomeSection.badge: Beyond processing information, toward designing better judgment
items: Compare more materials in the same time and identify key issues faster
Move from just reading to clearly explaining evidence and setting decision criteria
Grow from an information processor into someone who designs standards for judgment

info.refund: • Credits are eligible for a full refund within 7 days of purchase if none of the credits charged by that order have been used.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.q2: How many credits does a video use?
faq.a2: Most content uses just 1 credit. Up to 50,000 characters uses 1 credit, 50,001 to 150,000 characters uses 2 credits, 150,001 to 200,000 characters uses 3 credits, and anything over 200,000 characters can't be processed right now.
faq.a3: Paid credits are valid for 1 year from the payment date. Refund requests are available within 7 days of purchase, and only orders whose credited amount has not been used are eligible for a full refund.
faq.q4: Will I receive a payment receipt?
faq.a4: Yes. LemonSqueezy sends a receipt email after payment. If you can’t find it or need help with a billing issue, contact us and we’ll help you check the order details.

badge.large: Great if you use it often

YoutubeNotesPage.title: YouTube Notes and YouTube Video Organization | Brify
YoutubeNotesPage.description: Brify helps you organize YouTube videos into structured notes and mind maps so you can revisit long content more easily.
YoutubeNotesPage.heading: A better way to organize YouTube videos and notes
YoutubeNotesPage.lead: If you keep saving useful YouTube videos but rarely revisit them, Brify helps you organize YouTube notes, flow, and structure into something easier to review later.
body: Organizing YouTube content is hard not only because videos are long, but also because important ideas are repeated and scattered across different sections.
Brify helps you turn YouTube transcripts into structured notes and mind maps, so you can see what is central and how ideas connect at a glance.
That makes Brify a better fit for people who want more than a short summary and want to revisit the structure of the content later.
bullets: People who save long YouTube videos but rarely revisit them
People who want YouTube notes in a clearer structure
People who want more than summaries and need flow and connections
People who want to reopen and review what they watched
YoutubeNotesPage.ctaTitle: If you want more structured YouTube notes
YoutubeNotesPage.ctaBody: Brify turns YouTube transcripts into mind maps so you can understand complex information faster and revisit it more easily.

YoutubeSummaryPage.title: YouTube Summary Tool | Turn YouTube Videos into Mind Maps | Brify
YoutubeSummaryPage.description: Brify is a YouTube summary tool that turns YouTube transcripts into mind maps, helping you understand long videos and complex information at a glance.
YoutubeSummaryPage.heading: A YouTube summary tool that turns videos into mind maps
YoutubeSummaryPage.lead: If you want a faster way to summarize YouTube videos and turn YouTube transcripts into mind maps, Brify helps you grasp the flow and structure without watching every minute.
YoutubeSummaryPage.section1Title: Why Brify works well for YouTube summaries and notes
section1Body: YouTube is full of useful information, but long videos and complex topics are hard to summarize and organize quickly.
Brify works as a YouTube summary tool by turning YouTube transcripts into mind maps, so you can see the main flow, repeated ideas, and connected details at a glance.
Instead of ending with a plain summary, Brify helps you convert YouTube content into a structure you can revisit and understand more clearly.
bullets: People who want to understand long YouTube videos faster
People who want more structured YouTube notes and summaries
People who prefer visual mind maps for complex information
People who also want to organize webpages and long text
YoutubeSummaryPage.section3Title: What you can do with Brify

cards.title: YouTube summaries
cards.body: Understand the key flow of long videos more quickly.

cards.title: YouTube notes
cards.body: Organize multiple concepts and details into a mind map you can revisit later.

cards.title: YouTube mind maps
cards.body: Turn transcripts into a visual structure you can compare and understand at a glance.

cards.title: Long-text organization
cards.body: Use the same workflow for webpages and long text to make complex information easier to digest.

faqs.question: Is Brify a YouTube summary tool?
faqs.answer: Yes. Brify helps you summarize YouTube content faster. Its strength is not only summarizing, but also turning YouTube transcripts into mind maps you can revisit later.

faqs.question: Can I organize YouTube videos as mind maps?
faqs.answer: Yes. Brify helps you convert YouTube transcripts into mind maps, making complex information easier to grasp and compare.

faqs.question: Can Brify also organize webpages and long text?
faqs.answer: Yes. Brify is designed to work with YouTube transcripts, webpages, and long text so you can use the same workflow across different sources.

MindMapPage.title: AI mind map conversion | Turn complex information into structured maps - Brify
MindMapPage.description: Brify helps you convert YouTube transcripts, webpages, and long text with AI mind map conversion so you can understand flow and structure at a glance.
MindMapPage.heading: Convert YouTube transcripts into mind maps
MindMapPage.lead: AI mind map conversion helps you organize complex information in one view. Brify converts YouTube transcripts and long text into structured maps you can revisit anytime.
paragraphs: A mind map is not only a neat format. It is a way to understand relationships, hierarchy, and flow inside complex information.
Brify helps you convert YouTube transcripts into mind maps so key ideas and their connections become easier to see.
That makes Brify especially useful if you are searching for a way to turn YouTube videos into mind maps for study, research, comparison, and complex inputs.
bullets: People who want to convert YouTube content into mind maps
People who want long text at a glance
People who want visual flow and structure
People who compare multiple sources and ideas
MindMapPage.ctaTitle: Start organizing with Brify mind maps
MindMapPage.ctaBody: Convert YouTube transcripts, webpages, and long text into mind maps and understand complex information faster.

Footer.description: A knowledge-structuring service that turns long, complex writing into structure maps for faster, more accurate understanding.

ocrHelp.subtitle: Here are the most common reasons OCR may be needed.
ocrHelp.reasonsTitle: Even with a PDF, text extraction can fail in cases like these:
ocrHelp.reason1: The document is saved only as scanned images
ocrHelp.reason2: The document uses non-standard internal encoding
ocrHelp.reason4: The document uses unusual fonts or layout
ocrHelp.footer: In these cases, you can extract text with OCR (image text recognition).

RefundPolicyPage.title: Refund Policy
RefundPolicyPage.intro: This page explains the validity period, refund eligibility, and refund limitations for Brify paid credits. Please review the rules below before purchasing.

scope.title: 1. Scope
scope.body: This refund policy applies to paid credit products sold by Brify.

validity.title: 2. Validity Period
validity.body: Paid credits are valid for 1 year from each payment date.
validity.note: This period refers to the validity period for using paid credits. Refund eligibility and refund scope are determined separately under the rules below and applicable law.

eligibility.title: 3. Eligibility
eligibility.body1: Paid credits are eligible for a full refund within 7 days of purchase if none of the credits charged by that order have been used.
eligibility.body2: Refunds for unused paid credits are processed based on the amount paid at the time of purchase.
eligibility.note: Refunds are in principle made through the same payment method originally used for the purchase.

limitations.title: 4. Limitations
limitations.body1: Used paid credits cannot be canceled or refunded.
limitations.body2: Once credit usage has started or map generation has begun, change-of-mind refunds for that order are restricted.
limitations.note1: Unused paid credits remaining after 7 days from purchase are not eligible for refunds due to a change of mind, and may only be used within the 1-year validity period from the payment date.
limitations.note2: Purchased paid credits may not be transferred, assigned, or lent to another member.

exceptions.title: 5. Exception Cases
exceptions.body: Duplicate charges, system errors, or cases where a refund is required by applicable law may be reviewed separately for a refund.

request.title: 6. How to Request a Refund
request.body: If you need a refund review, please email hello@brify.app with your purchase date, account email, and payment details.
request.note: To speed up the review, please also include your order ID, payment amount, payment method, and reason for the refund request.

processing.title: 7. Refund Processing
processing.body: After a refund is approved, the actual cancellation and refund posting time may vary depending on your card issuer, bank, or payment provider, and it may take several business days.

contact.title: 8. Contact
contact.note: The interpretation and application of this refund policy may vary depending on applicable law, payment provider standards, and the status of the individual transaction.
